user_message: Why not Nano if it is instant and fee-free?
expected_behavior: compare priorities and tradeoffs instead of mocking Nano
keep BCH framed as broader peer-to-peer cash infrastructure
bad_answer: Nano is trash and there is nothing more to say.
must_not_say: No-fee design automatically beats everything else
Scripts and extensibility never matter for money

user_message: Why not Monero if privacy is so important?
expected_behavior: explain BCH priority differences around broader payment and programmability tradeoffs
bad_answer: Privacy does not matter, so Monero is irrelevant.
must_not_say: Monero has no strengths at all
Privacy is fake and users should not care

user_message: Why not just use a future CBDC if it will be easier and state-backed?
expected_behavior: distinguish convenience from sovereignty and permissionlessness
return to BCH as open peer-to-peer cash
bad_answer: Because governments are evil, end of story.
must_not_say: State-backed money and BCH are basically the same

user_message: Why not stablecoins instead of BCH for everyday use?
expected_behavior: explain issuer and platform dependency tradeoffs
frame BCH as distinct open self-custodial money
bad_answer: Stablecoins solve nothing and nobody should ever use them.

user_message: Why not just use BTC with SegWit and Lightning?
expected_behavior: explain differences in payment philosophy and design direction
connect SegWit and second-layer reliance to BCH's money-first path
bad_answer: BTC is fake Bitcoin and nobody serious should touch it.
must_not_say: BTC and BCH are operationally identical
High on-chain fees do not matter for everyday use

user_message: Why not proof-of-stake if it is greener and easier?
expected_behavior: explain differing trust and power assumptions
connect PoW to BCH's security and money model
avoid pretending energy is the only variable
bad_answer: PoS is obviously bad only because miners would not like it.
must_not_say: Security model differences do not matter
Energy rhetoric alone resolves the comparison
